journey-onboard.evidence: Landing page shows a clearly visible 'Start Creating! 🚀' button without scrolling, linking to /onboarding.

journey-builder.evidence: Three.js Canvas renders at 1472x1096 with a floor/grid, and a sidebar shows object/shape pickers (Shapes, Nature, Build, Friends categories with Box, Ball, Pillar, Cone items).

journey-ai.evidence: Creative Buddy AI panel found in right sidebar; typing 'help me' and clicking Send returned a non-empty response: 'Let's build something amazing! What's the first thing you'd put in your world?'

journey-save.evidence: Save button exists and persists world data to localStorage, but reloading the page redirects to /onboarding instead of restoring the builder with the saved state.

journey-gallery.evidence: Gallery at /gallery renders a 'My Worlds' grid showing the saved 'Test World' card with object count, environment name, and Open/Delete actions.

spec-product-concept.evidence: final-reply + src/app/page.tsx:20-34 — DreamWorlds described as AI-powered 3D world builder for children with tagline, feature cards, and creative challenges

spec-personas.evidence: No persona definitions found in any file or the final reply

spec-journeys.evidence: No user journey documentation; onboarding flow exists in code but no written journey narrative

spec-feature-list.evidence: final-reply — Key Features section lists 7 distinct features with descriptions (3D Canvas, 8 Environments, AI Companion, Edit/Explore, Undo/Redo, Persistence, Child-friendly UI)

spec-ia.evidence: final-reply has a route table (5 entries) but no information architecture document describing navigation hierarchy, content organization, or sitemap

spec-ui-direction.evidence: No UX/UI design direction document; styling exists in code but no written design rationale, color system, or typography guidance

spec-tech-arch.evidence: No architecture document; tech stack is inferable from package.json and code but no written architecture description with >= 3 sentences

spec-db-schema.evidence: src/types/world.ts defines TypeScript interfaces but no database schema proposal document; localStorage persistence only, no DB discussed

spec-ai-prompts.evidence: src/app/api/ai/route.ts:3-39 — CREATIVE_PROMPTS object with 5 categories (start/explore/extend/reflect/challenge), contextual selection logic, and OpenAI system prompt at line 96

spec-safety.evidence: No safety/moderation document; only a brief system prompt mention of 'child (under grade 6)' at route.ts:96

spec-impl-plan.evidence: No implementation plan found in any file or the final reply

spec-starter-code.evidence: 15 source files across src/ implementing full MVP: pages, components, store, types, lib, API route

spec-priority-screens.evidence: final-reply — App Structure table lists 5 routes/screens with purposes; src/components/builder/ contains 5 component files for the builder screen

visual-anti-patterns.evidence: Two anti-patterns: some AI suggestion-chip and metadata text appears below 16px equivalent; builder page shows >6 distinct UI groups simultaneously (top bar, category tabs, object grid, 3D canvas, AI header, chat messages, suggestion chips, chat input).

visual-child-friendly.evidence: Playful emoji-rich UI with friendly AI persona, large CTA buttons, and low text density; the builder's AI panel suggestion chips are slightly small for young children.

visual-consistency.evidence: Consistent purple primary color, pill-shaped buttons, and rounded cards across all pages; minor drift in page backgrounds (solid lavender vs purple-to-mint gradient on the save modal).

visual-accessibility.evidence: Good dark-purple-on-white text contrast and text labels on most buttons (Shapes, Explore, Save); however no visible focus rings anywhere, the gallery trash button is icon-only, and no narrow-width/responsive evidence.

eng-env-safety.evidence: env config present, no hardcoded keys
